skill.name: air-cryptographer
skill.description: Expert guidance for designing, reviewing, and auditing Algebraic Intermediate Representations in zero-knowledge proof systems.
skill.summary: Review AIR constraint systems for soundness, trace design, and proof-system risks.
skill.author: ClementWalter

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged external command, weak cryptography, and system reconnaissance patterns, but review found documentation-only markdown with mathematical notation and cryptography terminology. No executable code, network behavior, filesystem access, secret access, or prompt injection attempt was found in the scanned files.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Inline Mathematical Notation Detected as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: Static analysis treated markdown backticks around formulas, checklist entries, and constraint names as Ruby or shell backtick execution. These lines are documentation examples, not executable code, so no command execution path is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The affected files are markdown documentation, and the backticks mark inline formulas or code-style names. There is no script, command runner, interpreter invocation, or user input flow.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Cryptography Terms Detected as Weak Algorithms
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged weak cryptographic algorithm patterns, but the lines discuss AIR, STARK, FRI, Fiat-Shamir, trace design, and checklist labels. No implementation chooses DES, MD5, RC4, or another weak primitive.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The references are prose headings, descriptions, and table text about proof-system review. No code implements or recommends a weak cryptographic algorithm.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Reconnaissance Pattern in Review Prose
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged system reconnaissance, but the cited lines describe output constraints, lookup table columns, and deterministic step relations. No system information command or host inspection behavior appears.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines are natural-language checklist content. I found no uname, whoami, environment enumeration, network probing, or similar reconnaissance command.

content.value_statement: AIR soundness bugs can invalidate zero-knowledge proofs even when code appears correct. This skill guides structured constraint reviews, adversarial tests, and remediation planning.
actual_capabilities: Reviews AIR trace columns for missing constraints and free prover variables.
Checks transition, boundary, selector, range, and booleanity constraints.
Analyzes lookup, permutation, and memory consistency arguments for soundness gaps.
Builds adversarial witness tests for selector bypasses, last-row escapes, and product-zero attacks.
Produces review deliverables such as column inventories, constraint maps, degree tables, and ranked findings.
limitations: Does not prove formal security or replace specialist cryptographic review.
Requires the user to provide AIR specifications, constraints, or design notes.
Does not execute prover code or validate implementation correctness by itself.
Focuses on AIR and STARK-style systems, not general blockchain application security.

use_cases.title: Pre-Audit Constraint Review
use_cases.description: Find missing constraints, weak selectors, and incomplete boundary conditions before a formal audit.

use_cases.title: Design Soundness Check
use_cases.description: Evaluate a proposed AIR design before implementation work becomes expensive.

use_cases.title: Audit Report Preparation
use_cases.description: Organize findings into column inventories, constraint maps, degree tables, and remediation steps.

prompt_templates.title: Review AIR Basics
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of a small constraint system
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this AIR design for missing columns, boundary constraints, and transition constraints. Explain each risk in simple terms.

prompt_templates.title: Check Selector Soundness
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate review focused on gated constraints
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit these selectors and gated constraints. Look for all-zero selector states, overlapping modes, and unconstrained flags.

prompt_templates.title: Analyze Global Arguments
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review of lookups, permutations, and memory checks
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these lookup, permutation, and memory consistency arguments. Identify collision, omission, duplication, and product-zero risks.

prompt_templates.title: Build Adversarial Witness Tests
prompt_templates.prompt: Create adversarial witness exercises for this AIR. Include selector bypasses, last-row attacks, unconstrained columns, and degree-budget risks.

output_examples.input: Review a CPU trace AIR with opcode selectors and a halt flag.
output_examples.output: The review identifies selector coverage as the primary risk, then recommends booleanity checks, exclusive opcode selection, and a pinned halt row.

output_examples.input: Analyze a memory consistency argument that sorts by address and timestamp.
output_examples.output: The output highlights missing monotonic timestamp enforcement, read-before-write ambiguity, and the need to bind memory rows to the CPU trace.

output_examples.input: Prepare audit deliverables for a lookup-heavy STARK design.
output_examples.output: The response provides a column inventory, a constraint-to-claim map, degree risks, adversarial test cases, and prioritized fixes.

best_practices: Provide the trace schema, all constraints, public inputs, and transcript binding details.
Ask for adversarial examples, not only a checklist-style review.
Validate each proposed fix with a specific semantic claim and row set.
anti_patterns: Do not treat documented behavior as enforced behavior without a matching constraint.
Do not ignore padding, halt rows, or last-row transition behavior.
Do not reuse challenges across arguments without explicit domain separation.

faq.question: What is AIR?
faq.answer: AIR means Algebraic Intermediate Representation. It describes execution traces and polynomial constraints for STARK-style proof systems.

faq.question: Can this skill audit production ZK systems?
faq.answer: It can support review work, but it does not replace a full professional cryptographic audit.

faq.question: What inputs should I provide?
faq.answer: Provide trace columns, transition constraints, boundary constraints, public inputs, lookup arguments, and transcript challenge details.

faq.question: Does it run prover or verifier code?
faq.answer: No. The skill is documentation and reasoning guidance. It does not execute prover or verifier implementations.

faq.question: Can it find unconstrained columns?
faq.answer: Yes. It is designed to map each semantic claim to the constraint that enforces it.

faq.question: Does it support Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists compatibility with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
